correction.rationale: Use SAM point refinement rather than a polygon because the toes and ankle/instep have detailed curved boundaries. Positive points cover heel, midfoot, instep, and toe region; negative points suppress the gray background, the erroneous upper triangular extension, and neighboring/non-target areas.

judgments.evidence: Fail: the target identity is consistent with right_foot_base, but the proposed mask has background contamination, missing visible upper-foot area, and an abnormal fragmented/rectangular topology. The lower toe outline is mostly usable, so this is a refinement problem rather than a wrong-side problem.

observations.contour: The cyan contour follows the lower toe and sole outline reasonably well, but the upper contour contains straight, angular jumps and crosses into background rather than following the visible ankle/instep edge.
observations.full_context: The candidate is the character's right foot according to the supplied COCO semantic pose-chain evidence. The visible right foot is the foreground foot extending toward the viewer-right; the rear foot is a separate neighboring part.
observations.mask: The binary mask has large irregular holes and disconnected-looking regions over the upper foot/ankle, while also extending into non-foot regions. Its topology is implausible for one visible foot.
observations.neighbor_overlap: The mask appears to overlap gray background and may approach the adjacent/rear leg-foot area at the left/top; it should remain limited to the visible foreground right foot and not include the neighboring limb.
observations.overlay: The red overlay covers much of the lower foot and toes, but includes a conspicuous triangular gray-background area above the instep and leaves substantial visible upper-foot/ankle pixels uncovered.
observations.source_crop: The foot and toes are clearly visible, but the ankle/instep boundary is partly adjacent to gray background and the foot has strong visible pixel detail that should be covered continuously.
